votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the string length is at least 3 and ensuring that every contiguous subsegment of length 3 consists of distinct characters.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the string length is at least 3 and ensuring no three consecutive characters contain duplicates.
The Python code correctly implements the string length check and the sliding window of size 3 to ensure all consecutive triplets contain distinct characters, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It checks that the string length is at least 3 and that for every consecutive triple of characters, all three are distinct. The Coq spec defines 'happy' as length >= 3 and all consecutive triples have distinct characters, and is_happy_spec checks if res equals this judgment. The Python implementation returns the same boolean result for all concrete string inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It checks that the string length is at least 3 and that for every consecutive triple of characters, all three characters are distinct. The Coq spec uses `happy` as a Prop with the same conditions (length >= 3 and all consecutive triples have distinct characters), and `is_happy_spec` checks if the result matches this property. The Python implementation returns True iff the string is 'happy' by these criteria, and `is_happy_spec` correctly checks if `res` equals this computed value.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It checks that the string length is at least 3 and that for every consecutive triple of characters, all three characters are distinct. The Coq spec uses `happy` as a Prop with the same conditions (length >= 3 and all consecutive triples have distinct characters), and `is_happy_spec` checks that the result matches this property. The Python implementation returns True iff the string satisfies these conditions, matching the Coq semantics for concrete inputs.
Both require length >= 3 and ensure every consecutive triple has pairwise distinct characters; the spec check returns True iff res equals the happy(s) result.

Both require length >= 3 and ensure every consecutive triple of characters is pairwise distinct; the Python loop over range(len(s)-2) matches Coq’s i+2 < length, and is_happy_spec equates res with the computed happy result.

Both define happy as len>=3 and every length-3 window has pairwise distinct chars; Python iterates exactly those indices and returns the same boolean. is_happy_spec checks res equals happy(s), matching res = true <-> happy s.
